versions.prompt: Very dark abstract texture for a card background. Subtle weathered wood grain pattern blended with topographic contour lines. Dark warm tones — deep brown-black (#1A1510) fading to near-black (#0D0B08). A hint of amber warmth in the midtones. Must be very dark (90%+ dark) so white text is readable on top. Feels like a worn trail map or a lodge wall. Organic, rustic, not digital or geometric.
versions.analysis: Excellent dark texture with visible wood grain and topo line pattern. Amber warmth comes through in the midtones. Dark enough for text overlay. The worn trail map feel is strong — rustic and organic.
versions.iterate_notes: The topo lines could be slightly more subtle. Overall very usable as-is.

versions.prompt: Waxed canvas/oilskin texture with faint topo lines, dark warm tones.
versions.analysis: Great waxed canvas look with natural creases and folds. The topo lines are more subtle than v0. Feels like a well-worn Filson bag.
versions.iterate_notes: Try dark leather with embossed topo map lines for a more premium feel.

versions.prompt: Dark leather with embossed topo map lines, premium texture, deep brown-black.
versions.analysis: Very premium embossed leather look. The topo lines feel pressed into the surface. Rich and tactile.
versions.iterate_notes: Try shou sugi ban (burnt cedar) texture — charred wood could be very Colorado mining country.

versions.prompt: Shou sugi ban (burnt cedar) texture, charred wood surface with warm amber peeking through cracks.
versions.analysis: Amazing charred surface texture with warm amber peeking through the burned wood. Visually striking and uniquely Colorado mining country.
versions.iterate_notes: Try reclaimed barn wood planks with nail holes — rustic cabin feel with more visible wood character.

versions.prompt: Reclaimed barn wood planks with nail holes, dark weathered surface, mining cabin feel.
versions.analysis: Perfect mining cabin feel. Warm and rustic with visible nail holes and weathered plank joints. Distinctly Colorado heritage.
versions.iterate_notes: Could try blending the barn wood with faint topo lines from v0 for the best of both worlds. Or add a subtle snow dusting.
